text.title: react-hooks Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for react-hooks as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Reading ref.current inside JSX or during the render function body.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The value may differ between React's two rendering passes (Concurrent Mode), causing visual inconsistencies.

failure-mode.description: Omitting a variable from the dependency array to avoid re-running the effect — fixing with a functional state updater instead.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Stale closure: the effect captures an old value of count. Use the functional updater form to read the current value without declaring it as a dependency.

failure-mode.description: Passing an object or array literal as a dependency — it creates a new reference every render, causing the effect to re-run continuously.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Object identity comparison: {} !== {} even with equal contents. Use primitive deps or memoize the object with useMemo.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The memoization overhead exceeds the cost of the computation; just calculate inline.

failure-mode.description: Calling hooks conditionally based on a prop or runtime flag.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: React relies on a stable hook call order across renders. Conditional hooks violate the Rules of Hooks and cause runtime errors.
